pattern:product-workflow-coordination.title: Product Workflow Coordination
pattern:product-workflow-coordination.rule: Core business capability for coordinating product workflows, spanning task storage, reconciliation, and state management. Involves server-side coordination, UI interaction, CLI invocation, and multi-layer store access for task creation, indexing, and

pattern:report-management.title: Report Management
pattern:report-management.rule: Business capability for presenting business information in review and operations-friendly formats. Integrates task factory, legacy reconciliation, path normalization, and git metadata inference to generate actionable reports.

pattern:search-and-filtering.title: Search and Filtering
pattern:search-and-filtering.rule: Business capability for narrowing and filtering insight data into business-relevant views. Combines server-side filtering, SQLite indexing, markdown storage, dependency graph analysis, and tool-level query operations.

pattern:cross-module-bridges.title: Surprising Cross-Module Connections
pattern:cross-module-bridges.rule: Architectural anti-pattern documenting 10 unexpected cross-module bridges between distant subsystems. These connections indicate areas where architectural boundaries may be weak or where coupling could be reduced through better abstraction layers.

pattern:git-post-merge-task-linking.title: Git Post-Merge PR Task Linking Detection
pattern:git-post-merge-task-linking.rule: Git hook pattern extracting PR metadata from merge commits and automatically linking tracked tasks. Parses PR number from commit message, extracts task IDs from PR body and branch name using regex, and auto-transitions linked tasks to done via task-l

pattern:observability.title: Observability & Logging
pattern:observability.rule: Observability pattern with medium severity: present in 6 locations (task types, store, UI activity view, stats tool) but absent from 6 others (config loading, hooks, test utilities). Standardize logging via activity log, expose metrics via task-stats

pattern:performance.title: Performance Optimization
pattern:performance.rule: Performance pattern with medium severity: FTS5 indexing, debounced file watchers, per-project setImmediate chunking to avoid blocking, TanStack Query caching on UI. Benchmarks verify 500-task operations performance; focus on reconciliation latency an

pattern:non-fatal-error-silent-fallback.title: Non-fatal error fallback with silent exit 0
pattern:non-fatal-error-silent-fallback.rule: Post-merge hook uses try-catch blocks with silent exit 0 for multiple non-fatal error conditions, allowing graceful degradation when optional operations fail. This pattern prevents cascading failures in merge workflows.

pattern:static-file-path-traversal-guard.title: Static-file path traversal guard using path.resolve()
pattern:static-file-path-traversal-guard.rule: Server-side UI path validation uses path.resolve() with startsWith() guard to prevent directory traversal attacks on static file serving. This is a high-severity security boundary.

pattern:unversioned-binary-rename-risk.title: Missing grace period for mcp-agent-tasks binary rename
pattern:unversioned-binary-rename-risk.rule: The mcp-agent-tasks binary rename is a clean-cut migration without a grace period or backwards-compatibility alias, creating risk of breaking existing user scripts and documentation references.

pattern:hardcoded-array-index-binary-res.title: Hardcoded magic index [0] in binary resolution without platform-specific fallback
pattern:hardcoded-array-index-binary-res.rule: Binary resolution logic hardcodes array index [0] without platform-specific validation or fallback, creating fragility when array order changes or on different operating systems.

pattern:windows-path-resolution-fallback.title: Windows path resolution fallback to node_modules/.bin/ with space handling
pattern:windows-path-resolution-fallback.rule: Binary resolution uses fallback chain from system path to node_modules/.bin/, with execFileSync array-based argument passing to handle Windows paths containing spaces safely.

pattern:sqliteindex-god-object.title: SqliteIndex god object owning schema init, migration, and CRUD
pattern:sqliteindex-god-object.rule: SqliteIndex class exhibits god object anti-pattern by owning schema initialization, database migrations, and all CRUD operations. This violates separation of concerns and should be refactored into distinct layers.

pattern:esm-interop-fallback.title: ESM interop fallback if tdd-guard-vitest ships CJS-only
pattern:esm-interop-fallback.rule: Vitest upgrade path includes ESM interop fallback strategy for CJS-only tdd-guard-vitest, using dynamic imports and esModuleInterop to bridge module format incompatibility.

pattern:default-tasks-dir-sentinel.title: DEFAULT_TASKS_DIR_NAME sentinel constant for config fallback
pattern:default-tasks-dir-sentinel.rule: DEFAULT_TASKS_DIR_NAME sentinel constant is used throughout the codebase as a fallback when configuration field is missing, providing a single source of truth for default directory naming.

pattern:nullish-coalescing-optional-fiel.title: Nullish coalescing fallback for optional database fields
pattern:nullish-coalescing-optional-fiel.rule: Optional database fields (milestone, claimed_by, branch) use ?? null fallback pattern for safe null coalescing, providing consistent handling of missing optional data.

pattern:hardcoded-project-paths-sentinel.title: Hardcoded project path mapping with Windows paths
pattern:hardcoded-project-paths-sentinel.rule: High-severity sentinel: hardcoded project path dictionary (COND, ACR, HRLD, HBOOK, EXTR, PRSM, MCPAT) with full Windows paths, plus hardcoded MCP CLI path. This creates environmental brittleness and portability issues.

pattern:config-and-path-resolution-fallb.title: Config and path resolution fallback chains
pattern:config-and-path-resolution-fallb.rule: Multi-level fallback chains resolve tasks directory through explicit path → prefix match → cwd match → unknown, providing graceful degradation across configuration sources.

pattern:default-configuration-sentinels.title: DEFAULT_TASKS_DIR_NAME and DEFAULT_CONFIG sentinel constants
pattern:default-configuration-sentinels.rule: Two sentinel constants provide fallback defaults: DEFAULT_TASKS_DIR_NAME for directory naming and DEFAULT_CONFIG object with hardcoded default values for all configuration fields.

pattern:nullish-coalescing-field-backfil.title: Optional config field backfilling with nullish coalescing
pattern:nullish-coalescing-field-backfil.rule: Configuration loader uses ?? operator chains to backfill optional fields with defaults, providing consistent fallback behavior across missing configuration values.

pattern:magic-number-string-limits.title: Magic numbers for line and string length limits
pattern:magic-number-string-limits.rule: Frontmatter extraction uses multiple magic numbers: 50 for H1 search line limit, 200 for title string length limit, 2000 for body preview truncation, and 30/3 in regex patterns. These should be named constants.

pattern:hardcoded-timeout-constants.title: Hardcoded subprocess timeout and request limit magic numbers
pattern:hardcoded-timeout-constants.rule: Multiple hardcoded magic numbers for operational limits: 300 for PR list limit, 30000ms and 15000ms for subprocess timeouts, 120000ms for LLM subprocess timeout. These should be centralized configuration.

pattern:silent-fallback-on-command-failu.title: Silent fallback returning empty result on gh/git command failure
pattern:silent-fallback-on-command-failu.rule: Commands that fail (listMergedPrs, findPrByBranch) silently return empty array or undefined, enabling graceful degradation when external tools are unavailable or network connectivity is limited.

pattern:default-branch-detection-fallbac.title: Multi-level default branch detection fallback chain
pattern:default-branch-detection-fallbac.rule: Default branch detection uses fallback chain: origin/HEAD → main check → master check, providing robustness across different Git repository configurations and naming conventions.

pattern:magic-array-indices-git-parsing.title: Magic array indices in git output parsing
pattern:magic-array-indices-git-parsing.rule: Git output parsing uses magic array indices like parts[parts.length - 1] to extract values from split git command output. This is fragile to output format changes and should use structured parsing.

pattern:shell-detection-and-timeout-sent.title: Git Bash path normalization and subprocess timeout sentinels
pattern:shell-detection-and-timeout-sent.rule: High-severity sentinel: hardcoded Git Bash path normalization pattern for Windows shell detection, plus 120000ms hardcoded subprocess timeout for LLM execution. Both are environment-specific values that should be configurable.

pattern:environment-variable-fallback-ch.title: Environment variable and JSON parse error fallbacks
pattern:environment-variable-fallback-ch.rule: Process environment fallback uses HOME/USERPROFILE chain for isolated environment setup. Silent catch block on JSON parse errors implicitly falls through to keyword matching, creating implicit error handling path.

pattern:fallback-with-nullish-coalesce.title: Fallback using ?? operator for optional values
pattern:fallback-with-nullish-coalesce.rule: Widespread use of nullish coalescing operator (??) to provide default values when optional parameters are undefined or null. This pattern is used for path components, MIME types, timestamps, status fields, and configuration parameters. While function

pattern:hardcoded-sentinel-constants.title: Hardcoded sentinel values and magic constants throughout the codebase
pattern:hardcoded-sentinel-constants.rule: Magic constants appear throughout the codebase including hardcoded MIME type mappings, debounce timeout (200ms), schema version numbers, task complexity bounds (1-10), claim TTL (4 hours), subtask limits (10), max depth (3), field length constraints

pattern:implicit-tool-registration.title: Implicit tool array registration with 23 modules requires manual synchronization
pattern:implicit-tool-registration.rule: MCP tool registration relies on an implicit TOOLS array (line 54) containing 23 module imports that must be manually kept in sync. This is error-prone and high-severity because adding a new tool to the codebase requires remembering to also register i

pattern:optional-field-defaults.title: Optional fields default to null or empty string in data model transformations
pattern:optional-field-defaults.rule: Data transformations like rowToMilestone() use coercion patterns (null → undefined via ??) and tools like task-link-pr use empty string defaults for optional fields (pr_title, base_branch). This pattern reflects the challenge of serializing optional

pattern:default-status-fallback.title: Missing status values default to 'open' or 'draft' status
pattern:default-status-fallback.rule: When status is undefined during create operations, fallback defaults are used: milestones default to 'open', auto-captured tasks and spec types default to 'draft'. This pattern centralizes status initialization but may hide incomplete input validatio

pattern:field-length-constraints.title: Magic number field length constraints without named constants
pattern:field-length-constraints.rule: Field length constraints are hardcoded magic numbers: title 200 chars, why 1000 chars, spec_file 500 chars. These values lack named constants for reuse and lack documentation explaining the rationale for chosen limits. Centralizing these as named con

pattern:configuration-environment-variab.title: Environment variables with filesystem path fallbacks configure system behavior
pattern:configuration-environment-variab.rule: Configuration pattern uses environment variables (MCP_TASKS_CONFIG) with fallback paths (~/.config/mcp-tasks/config.json). This allows runtime configuration discovery but may mask missing configuration by silently falling back to defaults, making it

pattern:hardcoded-task-limit-workaround.title: Hardcoded limit with in-memory filtering as API workaround
pattern:hardcoded-task-limit-workaround.rule: Uses hardcoded limit of 10000 with full in-memory filtering as a workaround for lack of native dependency filtering in index.listTasks(). This masks a capability gap in the underlying API and creates a silent performance cliff when data exceeds the l

pattern:undocumented-task-constraints.title: Architectural constraints documented but not enforced
pattern:undocumented-task-constraints.rule: Architectural limits (max 10 subtasks, 100 transitions, 50 commits) are documented in comments but not enforced at runtime. Lack of validation allows invalid states to propagate.

pattern:silent-error-swallowing-api.title: Silent error handling and missing field masking in API errors
pattern:silent-error-swallowing-api.rule: Two error handling anti-patterns in transcribeAudio: fallback using ?? {} masks missing message field allowing undefined access, and catch(() => {}) silently swallows errors without logging or rethrowing.

pattern:runtime-null-filtering.title: Runtime filtering of potentially null values in React render
pattern:runtime-null-filtering.rule: Guard condition filtering projects at render time implies some values may be null/undefined rather than prevented at source. Defers validation to presentation layer instead of data layer.

pattern:sentinel-empty-filter-state.title: Sentinel value maintains filter object key invariant
pattern:sentinel-empty-filter-state.rule: EMPTY_FILTERS sentinel used to reset state indicates architectural invariant that all filter keys must be present even when empty. Constrains valid state space through naming convention rather than type system.

pattern:silent-fetch-failure-dependency.title: Silent fetch failure dependencies in component initialization
pattern:silent-fetch-failure-dependency.rule: Multiple .catch(() => {}) blocks on fetch calls mask silent failures in project lookup and config loading, allowing uninitialized state with no error indication to user.

pattern:audio-format-extension-mismatch.title: Filename extension mismatch with actual audio MIME type
pattern:audio-format-extension-mismatch.rule: Default filename 'recording.wav' is inconsistent with actual audio/webm MIME type recorded in VoiceCapture component. May cause confusion in file handling and type detection.

pattern:unknown-enum-fallback-masking.title: Silent masking of unknown enum variants in style fallbacks
pattern:unknown-enum-fallback-masking.rule: Fallback patterns using ?? with default styles (cls, 'text-slate-400') mask type mismatches and new enum variants not present in color maps. Hides breaking changes silently at runtime.

pattern:architectural-object-shape-invar.title: Architectural invariants on object key presence enforced via guards
pattern:architectural-object-shape-invar.rule: Guard conditions imply architectural invariants that certain objects must always have all keys defined (e.g., filter object with project, milestone, label). Invariants are not expressed in type system.

pattern:optional-chaining-with-empty-fal.title: Optional chaining with empty array fallback for optional properties
pattern:optional-chaining-with-empty-fal.rule: Pattern task.labels?.slice(0, 3) ?? [] masks missing labels property and allows graceful degradation. Useful for rendering but masks whether data was actually loaded or is genuinely absent.

pattern:conditional-rendering-optional-d.title: Conditional rendering of optional date fields
pattern:conditional-rendering-optional-d.rule: Guard condition returning null indicates architectural invariant that dates may be absent and must be conditionally rendered. Dates are optional in data model.

pattern:unknown-error-type-masking.title: Unknown error type masking in error message fallback
pattern:unknown-error-type-masking.rule: Error message fallback using instanceof Error ? err.message : 'Transcription failed' masks non-Error thrown values and loses context, making debugging harder.

pattern:undefined-masking-in-hooks.title: Silent undefined masking in data query hooks
pattern:undefined-masking-in-hooks.rule: Fallback pattern data ?? [] in hook returns masks undefined query results and silently provides empty arrays. Makes it impossible to distinguish between empty results and loading/error states.

pattern:hardcoded-default-project-string.title: Hardcoded default project string literal
pattern:hardcoded-default-project-string.rule: Uses ?? operator with hardcoded 'default' string literal to mask missing or empty projects array. Magic string breaks abstraction and is fragile to refactoring.

pattern:platform-aware-env-deletion.title: Platform-aware environment variable deletion in tests
pattern:platform-aware-env-deletion.rule: Using delete process.env avoids Windows EINVAL error that would occur with undefined assignment. Platform-specific workaround required for correct test cleanup behavior.

pattern:test-environment-restoration-gua.title: Conditional test environment restoration with undefined checks
pattern:test-environment-restoration-gua.rule: Conditional environment variable cleanup and restoration checking savedConfig === undefined. Guards prevent errors during test teardown when original values were not captured.

pattern:dependency-array-validation-guar.title: Dependencies array validation in circular dependency tests
pattern:dependency-array-validation-guar.rule: Dependencies array validation guards checking for non-empty dependency lists before processing. Validates preconditions for circular dependency detection tests.

pattern:claim-state-validation.title: Claim state validation in concurrency tests
pattern:claim-state-validation.rule: Claim state guard checking claimed_by field for non-null comparison. Validates concurrent task claim state transitions in multi-process scenarios.

pattern:task-status-transition-validatio.title: Strict task status transition validation
pattern:task-status-transition-validatio.rule: Task status transition guards checking for specific state progression (todo→in_progress→done). Enforces task lifecycle invariants in integration tests.

pattern:milestone-result-validation.title: Milestone reconciliation result validation
pattern:milestone-result-validation.rule: Silent fallback when milestones.yaml is missing — reconcile() returns 0 without throwing. Allows tests to pass with missing configuration files, masking setup errors.

pattern:orphaned-entity-validation.title: Post-operation orphaned entity validation
pattern:orphaned-entity-validation.rule: Guards checking that getTask() returns null/non-null after operations like pruning and reconciliation. Validates state transitions in index rebuilding operations.

pattern:graceful-corrupt-file-skipping.title: Graceful failure recovery on corrupt file parsing
pattern:graceful-corrupt-file-skipping.rule: Reconciler gracefully continues when file parsing fails without throwing. Allows partial recovery from corrupted data but masks which files were problematic.

pattern:reference-count-validation.title: Reference count validation before assertion
pattern:reference-count-validation.rule: Guard condition checking references array length == 1 before assertions. Validates expected cardinality of reference results in sync tests.

pattern:hardcoded-test-port-sentinel.title: Hardcoded ephemeral port for CLI server tests
pattern:hardcoded-test-port-sentinel.rule: TEST_PORT = 14567 hardcoded for test isolation. Magic number breaks abstraction and may conflict with other services using that port.

pattern:process-cleanup-timeout-fallback.title: Timeout with SIGKILL fallback for stubborn process cleanup
pattern:process-cleanup-timeout-fallback.rule: 8000ms timeout for stdout read with SIGKILL fallback. Handles processes that don't exit cleanly, but hardcoded timeout may be insufficient on slow systems.

pattern:conditional-test-skip-on-build-a.title: Conditional test skip based on build artifact presence
pattern:conditional-test-skip-on-build-a.rule: UI_BUILT flag skips all tests if dist/ui not present. Hides build failures behind test skips rather than failing loudly.

pattern:silent-test-skip-on-missing-asse.title: Silent test skip on missing asset match
pattern:silent-test-skip-on-missing-asse.rule: Conditional test return when no asset match found silently skips test. Masks incomplete asset loading without indication to test runner.

pattern:module-level-test-directory-glob.title: Module-level temporary directory globals for test isolation
pattern:module-level-test-directory-glob.rule: Module-level tmpDir globals (tmpDirA, tmpDirB, tmpDirDb) created once for all tests. Shared state across tests creates coupling and makes test order matter.

pattern:sentinel-current-dir-path.title: Sentinel value for direct path writing in tests
pattern:sentinel-current-dir-path.rule: tasksDirName='.' sentinel value writes directly into project path instead of subdirectory. Magic string bypasses directory structure abstraction.

pattern:dynamic-tool-import-in-tests.title: Dynamic tool handler imports in async tests (technical debt)
pattern:dynamic-tool-import-in-tests.rule: Dynamic import of tool handlers (task-add-subtask.js, task-promote-subtask.js) inside async test functions. Prevents static analysis and creates dependency on runtime module resolution.

pattern:subtask-count-validation.title: Updated parent subtask count validation after operation
pattern:subtask-count-validation.rule: Guard condition on updatedParent?.subtasks checking length after promotion. Validates that promotion operation correctly updates parent task state.

pattern:hardcoded-error-code-sentinels.title: Hardcoded error code sentinels in test assertions
pattern:hardcoded-error-code-sentinels.rule: Error codes (NO_AUDIO, GROQ_NOT_CONFIGURED, MISSING_FIELDS, PROJECT_NOT_FOUND) hardcoded in assertions. Magic strings make refactoring error codes brittle and hide the error contract.

pattern:hardcoded-project-prefix-sentine.title: Hardcoded project prefix in tests
pattern:hardcoded-project-prefix-sentine.rule: Project prefix 'VCT' hardcoded in test. Magic string breaks abstraction and couples tests to specific project naming convention.

pattern:hardcoded-performance-thresholds.title: Hardcoded performance thresholds in perf tests
pattern:hardcoded-performance-thresholds.rule: Performance thresholds hardcoded (10ms for list, 10ms for search, 200ms for reconcile). Magic numbers are not configurable and may need adjustment for different hardware or load profiles.

pattern:empty-transcript-validation.title: Empty transcript validation before task creation
pattern:empty-transcript-validation.rule: Guard condition !transcript.trim() prevents empty task creation from transcription. Valid but places validation at presentation layer rather than domain layer.

pattern:async-loading-state-guard.title: Async loading state guard before aggregation
pattern:async-loading-state-guard.rule: Guard condition !isLoading before aggregating totals depends on async query completion. Prevents rendering incomplete data but creates loading state coupling.

pattern:activity-result-size-constraint.title: Activity endpoint result size constraint
pattern:activity-result-size-constraint.rule: Guard condition on data.length <= 50 for activity endpoint validation. Tests that API enforces result set size limit but constraint is implicit.

pattern:console-log-benchmark-reporting.title: Console.log for benchmark reporting instead of proper logging
pattern:console-log-benchmark-reporting.rule: Performance benchmarks use console.log for reporting results instead of a structured logging service. This creates a tech debt opportunity to integrate a proper logging system for test output and metrics collection.

pattern:environment-restoration-fallback.title: Environment variable restoration using Object.entries and undefined checks
pattern:environment-restoration-fallback.rule: Test cleanup pattern in afterEach uses Object.entries() with undefined checks to restore process.env to its original state. Demonstrates a safe fallback approach for environment isolation in unit tests.

pattern:hardcoded-test-sentinels-config-.title: Hardcoded test paths for storage and project directories
pattern:hardcoded-test-sentinels-config-.rule: Test fixtures use hardcoded sentinel paths (/fake/global/storage, /fake/project, /fake/local-project) to establish consistent test environments. Common pattern for avoiding filesystem dependencies in unit tests.

pattern:hardcoded-windows-paths-binary-r.title: Hardcoded Windows paths for testing binary resolution
pattern:hardcoded-windows-paths-binary-r.rule: Test fixtures contain hardcoded Windows paths (C:\Users\micha\AppData...) for validating platform-specific binary resolution logic. Represents platform-specific sentinel values in cross-platform test suites.

pattern:hardcoded-file-paths-passive-cap.title: Hardcoded file paths for passive capture testing
pattern:hardcoded-file-paths-passive-cap.rule: Test cases use hardcoded file paths (/some/project/scratchpads/my-feature-plan.md) as sentinel values for testing file capture and path resolution functionality.

pattern:date-now-uniqueness-suffix.title: Date.now() suffix for ensuring test fixture uniqueness
pattern:date-now-uniqueness-suffix.rule: Test fixtures append Date.now() to config paths (mcp-hook-nonexistent-{time}) to guarantee uniqueness across parallel test runs. Tech debt marker for potential race conditions in test isolation.

pattern:brittle-string-matching-tests.title: String matching on script source code is brittle
pattern:brittle-string-matching-tests.rule: Tests validate logic by matching against exact script source code strings, creating brittle assertions that break with minor formatting changes. Indicates opportunity to use AST-based or semantic validation instead.

pattern:manual-schema-validation.title: Manual schema property checking instead of using JSON Schema validator
pattern:manual-schema-validation.rule: Schema validation is performed manually via expect() assertions on schema.properties rather than using a dedicated JSON Schema validator library. Represents duplication of validation logic that could be centralized.

pattern:hardcoded-complexity-minimum.title: Hardcoded complexity minimum value in schema and factory
pattern:hardcoded-complexity-minimum.rule: Task factory enforces a complexity minimum of 1 via max(deps.length + 1, 1) calculation, with schema enforcing the same sentinel value. Represents a business rule encoded as a constant that should be defined once and referenced consistently.

pattern:spread-operator-fallback.title: Spread operator default fallback in factory helper
pattern:spread-operator-fallback.rule: The makeGit() helper uses spread operator with default values to construct GitInferenceResult objects, allowing partial specifications while providing fallback values for missing fields.

pattern:optional-chaining-undefined-fall.title: Optional chaining with undefined fallback in assertions
pattern:optional-chaining-undefined-fall.rule: Test uses optional chaining (v2!.done) pattern that may result in undefined, representing a defensive coding approach for optional nested property access.

pattern:sentinel-task-state-defaults.title: Sentinel task state with default status and priority
pattern:sentinel-task-state-defaults.rule: makeTask factory establishes sentinel defaults (status='todo', priority='medium') for consistent test fixture creation, defining canonical default values for task state.

pattern:valid-transitions-state-machine.title: VALID_TRANSITIONS object defining state machine transition rules
pattern:valid-transitions-state-machine.rule: Sentinel object that defines allowed state transitions for a state machine, establishing the single source of truth for valid workflow state changes.

pattern:optional-chaining-array-fallback.title: Optional chaining with nullish coalescing for array fallback
pattern:optional-chaining-array-fallback.rule: extractTypeUnion function uses optional chaining with nullish coalescing (values?.map(...) ?? []) to safely handle missing regex matches by providing empty array fallback.

decision:deferred-vitest-and-mcp-feature.title: Deferred vitest upgrade and MCP config hot-reload

decision:command-center-open-questions.title: Four open architectural questions deferred in command-center

decision:spec-task-type-design-questions.title: Five open questions on spec task type design

decision:incomplete-task-transition-impl.title: Manual verification required for BODY-MATCH task transition

flow:git-hooks-automation.title: Git Hooks and Zero-Friction Task Automation

rule:error-handling.title: Error Handling
rule:error-handling.rule: Error handling is consistently implemented across 5 present locations (types, tools, store, schema) but absent from 5 locations (config validation, git client, utilities). Critical rule for high-severity concern: standardize error codes, propagate st

rule:security.title: Security Validation
rule:security.rule: Security concern at critical severity: input validation present in 6 locations (schemas, tools, hooks) but gaps in 7 locations (git operations, CLI, config loading). Enforce schema validation at all entry points, sanitize git/GitHub output, validate

rule:resilience.title: Resilience & Recovery
rule:resilience.rule: High-severity resilience patterns: atomic writes with temp-file rename, index rebuild capability, reconciliation between disk and index, error recovery in hooks. Implemented consistently across storage layer (8 locations); requires documentation and

rule:git-hook-execution-guards.title: Fast-exit guards in global git hooks
rule:git-hook-execution-guards.rule: Global git hooks (post-commit, post-merge) enforce architectural invariants by checking for .mcp-tasks.json and agent-tasks/ directory existence before processing. These fast-exit guards are critical to prevent stale task states from accumulating.

rule:module-format-compatibility.title: CJS-only tdd-guard-vitest requires esModuleInterop
rule:module-format-compatibility.rule: The tdd-guard-vitest package ships as CommonJS-only without an exports map. Dynamic ESM import is confirmed working, but TypeScript's esModuleInterop flag must be enabled for named imports to function correctly.

rule:task-lifecycle-enforcement-gates.title: Task lifecycle enforcement guards checking collection emptiness
rule:task-lifecycle-enforcement-gates.rule: Multiple architectural guards enforce task lifecycle by checking for zero task calls, zero references, zero PR transitions, and zero state transitions. These guards reveal missing enforcement points in the task state machine that require redesign.

rule:sqlite-counter-initialization.title: SQLite counter initialization guard
rule:sqlite-counter-initialization.rule: Guard conditions on collection emptiness checking SQLite counter initialization are critical for ensuring nextId counter is properly seeded on first access, preventing duplicate ID generation.

rule:domain-invariant-status-transitions.title: Domain invariant guards on task status transitions and spec_file ownership
rule:domain-invariant-status-transitions.rule: High-severity guards enforce domain invariants on task status transition rules and spec_file field ownership constraints. These guards ensure data integrity across the task domain model.

rule:hook-execution-ordering-guards.title: Hook execution ordering guards for .mcp-tasks.json and agent-tasks directory
rule:hook-execution-ordering-guards.rule: Fast-exit guards in global hooks checking .mcp-tasks.json and agent-tasks directory must execute first to establish architectural prerequisites. Guard ordering is critical to prevent stale state propagation.

rule:schema-drift-validation-guard.title: Schema drift guard requiring markdown-store frontmatter validation
rule:schema-drift-validation-guard.rule: High-severity guard requiring markdown-store.read validation of generated frontmatter to detect schema drift between generated content and stored schema definitions.

rule:concurrent-init-atomic-write-guard.title: Atomic temp-file rename guard for concurrent init calls
rule:concurrent-init-atomic-write-guard.rule: Concurrent config initialization calls require atomic temp-file rename to prevent race conditions where multiple processes initialize the same configuration file simultaneously.

rule:config-projects-collection-guard.title: Config projects collection emptiness guard
rule:config-projects-collection-guard.rule: Guard condition checking if config.projects collection is empty before processing prevents iteration errors and null reference exceptions in multi-project configuration.

rule:cli-argument-parsing-guard.title: CLI flag presence and array length validation guard
rule:cli-argument-parsing-guard.rule: Guard condition checking both flag presence and resulting array length prevents array index out-of-bounds errors during CLI argument parsing for project, since, and limit flags.

rule:config-projects-array-access-guard.title: Config projects array length check before element access
rule:config-projects-array-access-guard.rule: Guard condition checking config.projects length before accessing first element prevents array index out-of-bounds errors when no projects are configured.

rule:project-existence-check-guard.title: Project existence check guard before storage property access
rule:project-existence-check-guard.rule: Guard condition checking project existence before accessing nested storage property prevents null reference exceptions in configuration initialization.

rule:gh-cli-availability-guard.title: GitHub CLI availability guard before command execution
rule:gh-cli-availability-guard.rule: Guard condition checking gh CLI availability (isGhAvailable) before executing commands prevents errors when GitHub CLI is not installed in the environment.

rule:git-deduplication-and-error-handlin.title: Git branch deduplication and command failure handling guards
rule:git-deduplication-and-error-handlin.rule: Two critical guards: (1) trimmed line and Set membership check for branch deduplication; (2) try-catch blocks silently swallowing git command failures for graceful degradation in default branch detection.

rule:guard-against-invalid-state.title: Guard conditions protect against invalid state transitions and operations
rule:guard-against-invalid-state.rule: Comprehensive defensive programming pattern using guard clauses to enforce constraints: type checking (Array.isArray), existence checks (instanceof Error), idempotency guards (early returns on duplicate state), subtask limits (max 10), nesting depth

rule:idempotency-guards.title: Idempotency guards prevent duplicate state from repeated operations
rule:idempotency-guards.rule: Multiple operations use idempotency guards: checking if .gitignore exists before creation, comparing branch before update, checking if commit SHA already exists before appending, and checking registration status before config persistence. This patter

rule:circular-dependency-detection.title: Circular dependency detection in task dependencies
rule:circular-dependency-detection.rule: Task dependencies must prevent circular references. Non-circular paths succeed, but updateTask and createTask must throw CIRCULAR_DEPENDENCY error when cycles are detected, including direct self-dependencies, longer chains, and new tasks that would c

rule:subtask-nesting-depth-limit.title: Maximum nesting depth of 3 levels prevents excessive subtask hierarchies
rule:subtask-nesting-depth-limit.rule: Depth constraint guard (if (parent.parent && grandparent?.parent)) prevents exceeding 3-level nesting hierarchy. This architectural constraint preserves query performance and UI usability by preventing pathologically deep task trees. Maximum subtask

rule:type-validation-sentinels.title: Validation uses sentinel Sets (VALID_TYPES, VALID_PRIORITIES) for enum enforcement
rule:type-validation-sentinels.rule: Input validation enforces allowed values via sentinel Sets (VALID_TYPES, VALID_PRIORITIES) rather than TypeScript enums or database constraints. This runtime validation pattern provides flexibility but creates duplication with database schema enums,

rule:perf-tests-conditional-execution.title: Conditional test skip via PERF_ENABLED environment flag
rule:perf-tests-conditional-execution.rule: Tests are conditionally skipped based on process.env.PERF_ENABLED flag. Establishes a guard pattern for gating performance test suites behind an environment variable, allowing selective execution of expensive test scenarios.

rule:guard-collection-emptiness.title: Guard on collection emptiness before assertions
rule:guard-collection-emptiness.rule: Test checks summary.scanned count before proceeding with assertions, establishing a guard pattern to prevent null reference errors when collections are empty.

rule:guard-existence-check.title: Guard condition checking property existence before method call
rule:guard-existence-check.rule: Test explicitly guards against null/undefined by checking stats.milestones existence before calling find(), preventing potential runtime errors on missing optional properties.

rule:max-depth-nesting-guard.title: MAX_DEPTH_EXCEEDED guard preventing infinite parent chain nesting
rule:max-depth-nesting-guard.rule: High-severity guard rule that checks parent chain depth to prevent infinite nesting cycles. Represents a critical safety constraint for hierarchical task structures.

rule:array-length-guard-type-completenes.title: Guard on array length to verify union type completeness
rule:array-length-guard-type-completenes.rule: Test uses array length assertions to verify that all expected union type members are present, ensuring exhaustiveness of type enumerations.

rule:array-length-guard-assertions.title: Guard on array length > 0 before union membership assertions
rule:array-length-guard-assertions.rule: Test guards array length before making assertions on union type membership, preventing invalid assertions on empty collections.

rule:action-button-endpoints.title: Action button endpoints
rule:action-button-endpoints.rule: API endpoints for action buttons must return project configuration. GET /api/projects returns the project list with file paths, GET /api/config returns conductor URLs from environment variables, and GET /api/config hides conductor URLs when not set.

rule:build-task-summary-idempotency.title: build-task-summary script output and idempotency
rule:build-task-summary-idempotency.rule: The build-task-summary script must produce .summary.jsonl output with one JSON line per task, include optional fields when present and exclude task body content, and remain idempotent—running twice produces identical output.

rule:concurrency-claim-conflicts.title: Concurrency: claim conflicts
rule:concurrency-claim-conflicts.rule: Task claiming is session-exclusive. A second session cannot claim a task already held by a first session. After the first session releases the task, the second session can claim it.

rule:install-command-idempotency.title: install command idempotency
rule:install-command-idempotency.rule: The install command must be idempotent. Dry-run must not write any files, installing twice produces exactly one passive-capture entry in PostToolUse, and --dry-run prints what would be done without writing files.

rule:task-lifecycle-states.title: Task lifecycle: create → claim → link-commit → link-pr (merged) → done → archive
rule:task-lifecycle-states.rule: Tasks follow a complete lifecycle state machine. A task is created with status=todo, then claimed by a session, linked to a commit, linked to a merged PR, verified as done, and finally archived.

rule:milestones-yaml-reconciliation.title: Reconciler reads milestones.yaml
rule:milestones-yaml-reconciliation.rule: The reconciler reads milestones.yaml fixture files and inserts milestone rows into the index. Reconciliation is idempotent, malformed YAML is silently skipped without throwing errors, and missing milestones.yaml files are gracefully skipped.

rule:rebuild-index-prune-orphans.title: rebuild-index --prune-orphans behavior
rule:rebuild-index-prune-orphans.rule: The pruneOrphans() function removes stale index rows that correspond to deleted markdown files after reconciliation completes.

rule:rebuild-sqlite-index.title: Rebuild SQLite index from markdown files
rule:rebuild-sqlite-index.rule: The reconciler rebuilds the SQLite index from markdown source files, restoring the index even after database deletion. It removes stale rows when files are deleted and skips corrupt markdown files while continuing to process valid ones.

rule:reference-sync-sqlite.title: reference-sync: task_references ↔ SQLite
rule:reference-sync-sqlite.rule: Task references must be synchronized between markdown and the task_references SQLite table. Creating a task with references syncs them to the table; re-upserting with empty references removes the corresponding rows.

rule:serve-ui-html-dashboard.title: serve-ui HTML dashboard endpoints
rule:serve-ui-html-dashboard.rule: The serve-ui command serves an HTML dashboard with React root mount point and Vite module script. GET / returns HTML containing the React mount point, Vite module script, and serves linked static assets.

rule:serve-ui-http-api.title: serve-ui HTTP API endpoints
rule:serve-ui-http-api.rule: The serve-ui HTTP server provides JSON API endpoints for /api/tasks, /api/milestones, /api/activity (limited to 50 items), and /api/stats. It supports filtering by auto_captured, returns 404 for missing resources, 400 for invalid input, and must clos

rule:subtask-promotion.title: Subtask promotion
rule:subtask-promotion.rule: Subtasks can be added to parent tasks via task_add_subtask and promoted to full independent tasks via task_promote_subtask. Multiple subtasks can coexist, and promoting one does not affect others.

rule:transcribe-endpoint-validation.title: POST /api/transcribe
rule:transcribe-endpoint-validation.rule: The /api/transcribe endpoint requires an audio file in the request body and returns 400 if no audio file is provided. It returns 500 if the GROQ_API_KEY environment variable is not set.

rule:voice-capture-endpoints.title: Voice capture task creation endpoints
rule:voice-capture-endpoints.rule: POST /api/tasks creates draft tasks from voice input. Title field is required (returns 400 if missing), body field stores task description text, and unknown project returns 404. Tasks are created in draft status with proper validation.

rule:resolve-server-db-path.title: resolveServerDbPath configuration lookup
rule:resolve-server-db-path.rule: The resolveServerDbPath function returns global .index.db for global-storage projects, local .index.db for local-storage projects, and defaults to global .index.db when no matching project entry is found in configuration.

rule:load-config-cwd-independence.title: loadConfig() cwd-independence
rule:load-config-cwd-independence.rule: The loadConfig() function ignores local .mcp-tasks.json in the current working directory when a global configuration file exists, ensuring global configuration takes precedence.

rule:pick-best-binary.title: pickBestBinary platform-specific binary selection
rule:pick-best-binary.rule: The pickBestBinary function selects the correct binary executable for the current platform. On Windows, it prefers .cmd extensions over bare POSIX scripts and falls back to bare paths when .cmd is absent. On non-Windows, it returns the first non-empt

rule:passive-capture-hook.title: passive-capture hook
rule:passive-capture-hook.rule: Hook that processes plan files with stderr markers, skips code changes without active tasks, handles malformed stdin gracefully, and requires global config presence.

rule:reconcile-stuck-tasks-script.title: reconcile-stuck-tasks script
rule:reconcile-stuck-tasks-script.rule: Script that retrieves merged PRs with url field populated and uses --pr-number flag when calling link-pr tool.

rule:task-schema-json.title: task.schema.json
rule:task-schema-json.rule: JSON schema includes plan in type enum, defines all new optional fields, and preserves all original required fields.

rule:config-file-hot-reload.title: Config file hot-reload
rule:config-file-hot-reload.rule: Configuration reload mechanism calls handler when config file changes with 500ms debounce.

rule:detect-cycle-function.title: detectCycle()
rule:detect-cycle-function.rule: Cycle detection algorithm identifies direct cycles, self-loops, transitive cycles, and complex graph patterns including diamond dependencies without false positives.

rule:manifest-writer-roundtrip.title: ManifestWriter.write() + read() roundtrip
rule:manifest-writer-roundtrip.rule: Manifest write/read round-trip preserves task counts and IDs, maintains all fields, computes subtask progress correctly, and reflects PR association via has_pr flag.

rule:manifest-writer-read-edge-cases.title: ManifestWriter.read() edge cases
rule:manifest-writer-read-edge-cases.rule: Manifest reader returns empty array when index.yaml is missing and produces valid YAML for empty task lists.

rule:markdown-store-read.title: MarkdownStore.read()
rule:markdown-store-read.rule: Markdown parser extracts task fields from fixtures, handles subtasks/commits/dependencies, and throws SCHEMA_MISMATCH for corrupt frontmatter and TASK_NOT_FOUND for missing files.

rule:markdown-store-write-roundtrip.title: MarkdownStore.write() roundtrip
rule:markdown-store-write-roundtrip.rule: Markdown write/read round-trip preserves all fields, updates timestamps, cleans temporary files, and prevents accidental overwrites of tasks with different IDs.

rule:markdown-store-frontmatter-caps.title: MarkdownStore.frontmatter cap enforcement
rule:markdown-store-frontmatter-caps.rule: Markdown frontmatter enforces data size limits: transitions capped at 100, git commits capped at 50 on write.

rule:markdown-store-labels-tags-alias.title: MarkdownStore.labels/tags alias + new fields
rule:markdown-store-labels-tags-alias.rule: Markdown store merges tags and labels into deduplicated array, serializes as labels with backward-compatible tags, and preserves references, auto_captured, and milestone fields.

rule:milestone-repository.title: MilestoneRepository
rule:milestone-repository.rule: Milestone repository supports CRUD operations: create/list/get/update/close/delete with status changes and orphan detection for tasks referencing nonexistent milestones.

rule:reconciler-prune-orphans.title: Reconciler.pruneOrphans()
rule:reconciler-prune-orphans.rule: Index pruning removes entries for missing markdown files while preserving entries whose files still exist on disk.

rule:reference-repository.title: ReferenceRepository
rule:reference-repository.rule: Reference repository supports adding/retrieving references from source and target tasks, removes all task references, rejects self-references, and detects direct circular references.

rule:sqlite-index-upsert-get.title: SqliteIndex.upsertTask() + getTask()
rule:sqlite-index-upsert-get.rule: Task upsert/retrieve round-trip preserves all fields including tags, subtasks, transitions, and git commits; updates are idempotent.

rule:sqlite-index-delete-task.title: SqliteIndex.deleteTask()
rule:sqlite-index-delete-task.rule: Task deletion removes task from index and cascades to subtask rows.

rule:sqlite-index-fts5-triggers.title: SqliteIndex.FTS5 triggers
rule:sqlite-index-fts5-triggers.rule: Full-text search triggers maintain index on insert, update, and delete operations, enabling title-based searchability.

rule:sqlite-index-claim-task-toctou.title: SqliteIndex.claimTask() TOCTOU
rule:sqlite-index-claim-task-toctou.rule: Task claim prevents double-claiming while allowing the same session to update TTL via re-claim.

rule:sqlite-index-get-next-task.title: SqliteIndex.getNextTask()
rule:sqlite-index-get-next-task.rule: Task scheduler returns highest-priority todo with resolved dependencies first, skips tasks with unresolved deps, and returns null when no ready tasks exist.

rule:sqlite-index-list-tasks.title: SqliteIndex.listTasks()
rule:sqlite-index-list-tasks.rule: Task listing filters by status, project, and auto_captured flag while respecting pagination limit.

rule:sqlite-index-next-id.title: SqliteIndex.nextId()
rule:sqlite-index-next-id.rule: ID generator starts at 1 per project, increments each call, and consults disk to avoid collisions with existing files when tasksDir provided.

rule:sqlite-index-release-task.title: SqliteIndex.releaseTask()
rule:sqlite-index-release-task.rule: Task release validates session ownership and returns false for mismatched sessions.

rule:sqlite-index-get-child-tasks.title: SqliteIndex.getChildTasks()
rule:sqlite-index-get-child-tasks.rule: Child task retrieval returns subtasks registered in children table.

rule:sqlite-index-get-stats.title: SqliteIndex.getStats()
rule:sqlite-index-get-stats.rule: Statistics endpoint returns task counts grouped by status.

rule:sqlite-index-schema-migration-step-.title: SqliteIndex.schema migration — new columns (Step 4)
rule:sqlite-index-schema-migration-step-.rule: Database schema migration adds milestone, estimate_hours, plan_file, auto_captured columns; creates milestones and task_references tables; init() is idempotent.

rule:sqlite-index-upsert-new-fields-step.title: SqliteIndex.upsertTask() — new fields roundtrip (Step 5)
rule:sqlite-index-upsert-new-fields-step.rule: New fields persist and retrieve correctly without spurious nulls when unset in task shape.

rule:store-registry.title: StoreRegistry
rule:store-registry.rule: Registry registers all projects from config, resolves stores by prefix and task ID, shares TaskStore for global-storage projects, and deduplicates task directories.

rule:task-factory-format-id.title: TaskFactory.formatId()
rule:task-factory-format-id.rule: ID formatter pads numbers 1–999 to 3 digits with zero-fill, uses full width for 1000+.

rule:task-factory-create.title: TaskFactory.create()
rule:task-factory-create.rule: Task factory initializes schema version 1, sets todo status, synchronized timestamps, null claim placeholders, file paths, complexity from dependency count (capped at 10), and optional template body.

rule:task-factory-humanize-slug.title: TaskFactory.humanizeSlug()
rule:task-factory-humanize-slug.rule: Slug humanizer capitalizes plain slugs, strips task-ID prefixes and file extensions, converts underscores to spaces.

rule:task-factory-from-capture-event.title: TaskFactory.fromCaptureEvent()
rule:task-factory-from-capture-event.rule: Capture event handler creates plan tasks with auto_captured=true and draft status, creates spec tasks with auto_captured=true, and rejects skip and code_change types.

rule:task-store-create-task.title: TaskStore.createTask()
rule:task-store-create-task.rule: Task creation writes to SQLite and markdown, formats IDs correctly with sequential increment, rejects circular dependencies, sets todo status, and avoids ID collisions with disk files.

rule:task-store-update-task.title: TaskStore.updateTask()
rule:task-store-update-task.rule: Task update rejects status changes, persists field changes to SQLite and markdown, marks complexity as manual when set, and returns task-not-found errors.

rule:task-store-transition-task.title: TaskStore.transitionTask()
rule:task-store-transition-task.rule: Task transition validates state changes, appends to transitions array, rejects invalid transitions, persists updates to SQLite and markdown.

rule:task-store-claim-task.title: TaskStore.claimTask()
rule:task-store-claim-task.rule: Task claim updates frontmatter with claimed_by, prevents double-claiming, uses default 4-hour TTL when not specified.

rule:task-store-release-task.title: TaskStore.releaseTask()
rule:task-store-release-task.rule: Task release allows subsequent claims by other sessions.

rule:task-init-validate.title: task_init.validate()
rule:task-init-validate.rule: Admin tool validation requires project_prefix, validates storage_mode enum, and passes with minimal valid configuration.

rule:task-init-execute-local-storage.title: task_init.execute() with local storage
rule:task-init-execute-local-storage.rule: Local storage initialization creates tasks/, archive/, and .gitignore directories and is idempotent.

rule:task-rebuild-index-execute.title: task_rebuild_index.execute()
rule:task-rebuild-index-execute.rule: Index rebuild validates project existence, runs reconciler, and returns rebuilt count.

rule:task-register-project-validate.title: task_register_project.validate()
rule:task-register-project-validate.rule: Project registration validation requires prefix, path, and valid storage mode.

rule:task-register-project-execute.title: task_register_project.execute()
rule:task-register-project-execute.rule: Project registration writes config and is idempotent; returns already_existed flag.

rule:task-create-validate.title: task_create.validate()
rule:task-create-validate.rule: Task creation validation requires object input with project, enforces title length ≤200 chars, validates type/priority enums, and limits tags to 10.

rule:task-create-execute.title: task_create.execute()
rule:task-create-execute.rule: Task creation delegates to store.createTask and returns the created task.

rule:task-get-validate.title: task_get.validate()
rule:task-get-validate.rule: Task retrieval validation requires id parameter.

rule:task-get-execute.title: task_get.execute()
rule:task-get-execute.rule: Task retrieval returns found task or error response for missing tasks.

rule:task-list-validate.title: task_list.validate()
rule:task-list-validate.rule: List validation accepts empty input, validates status enum, and enforces limit > 0.

rule:task-list-execute.title: task_list.execute()
rule:task-list-execute.rule: List execution delegates to index.listTasks with defaults and passes filters through.

rule:task-delete-execute.title: task_delete.execute()
rule:task-delete-execute.rule: Delete execution calls store.archiveTask and propagates errors; returns archived=true on success.

rule:task-search-execute.title: task_search.execute() returns filtered results
rule:task-search-execute.rule: The task_search.execute() function must return search results and support filtering by project when provided. This is a core query operation for finding tasks by text search.

rule:task-update-validate.title: task_update.validate() enforces field constraints
rule:task-update-validate.rule: The task_update.validate() function must enforce strict input validation: id is required, title cannot exceed 200 characters, priority must be valid, and complexity must be in range. It should pass with valid input.

rule:task-update-execute.title: task_update.execute() delegates to store
rule:task-update-execute.rule: The task_update.execute() function must call store.updateTask() and return the updated task result.

rule:task-next-validate.title: task_next.validate() requires project field
rule:task-next-validate.rule: The task_next.validate() function must throw an error when the project field is missing, enforcing that querying for the next task requires a project context.

rule:task-next-execute.title: task_next.execute() returns next available task
rule:task-next-execute.rule: The task_next.execute() function must return a task when available, or a response with available=false when no tasks are found in the project queue.

rule:task-link-commit-validate.title: task_link_commit.validate() requires id, sha, and message
rule:task-link-commit-validate.rule: The task_link_commit.validate() function must require id, sha, and message fields and pass validation only when all three are provided.

rule:task-link-commit-execute.title: task_link_commit.execute() is idempotent
rule:task-link-commit-execute.rule: The task_link_commit.execute() function must add a commit reference to a task, be idempotent (linking the same SHA twice does not create duplicates), and throw TASK_NOT_FOUND when the task does not exist.

rule:task-link-pr-validate.title: task_link_pr.validate() requires id, pr_number, and pr_state
rule:task-link-pr-validate.rule: The task_link_pr.validate() function must require id and pr_number, validate that pr_state is one of the allowed values, and only pass when all constraints are satisfied.

rule:task-link-pr-execute.title: task_link_pr.execute() auto-transitions on merge
rule:task-link-pr-execute.rule: The task_link_pr.execute() function must link a PR to a task and automatically transition the task to done when pr_state is 'merged', unless the task is already done. It must throw TASK_NOT_FOUND if the task does not exist.

rule:task-link-branch-validate.title: task_link_branch.validate() requires id and branch
rule:task-link-branch-validate.rule: The task_link_branch.validate() function must require both id and branch fields, passing validation only when both are provided.

rule:task-link-branch-execute.title: task_link_branch.execute() is idempotent
rule:task-link-branch-execute.rule: The task_link_branch.execute() function must link a branch to a task, be idempotent (relinking the same branch skips the store update), and throw TASK_NOT_FOUND when the task does not exist.

rule:task-milestone-tool.title: task_milestone tool supports CRUD operations
rule:task-milestone-tool.rule: The task_milestone tool must support create, list, close, get, and delete operations. Closing a milestone must set its status to 'closed', and deletion must remove it from the list.

rule:task-blocked-by-validate.title: task_blocked_by.validate() requires id
rule:task-blocked-by-validate.rule: The task_blocked_by.validate() function must require an id field and throw when it is missing.

rule:task-blocked-by-execute.title: task_blocked_by.execute() returns non-done dependencies
rule:task-blocked-by-execute.rule: The task_blocked_by.execute() function must return an empty list when no dependencies exist, return only non-done dependencies as blocking tasks, and throw TASK_NOT_FOUND when the task does not exist.

rule:task-unblocks-validate.title: task_unblocks.validate() requires id
rule:task-unblocks-validate.rule: The task_unblocks.validate() function must require an id field and throw when it is missing.

rule:task-unblocks-execute.title: task_unblocks.execute() returns dependent tasks
rule:task-unblocks-execute.rule: The task_unblocks.execute() function must return tasks that depend on the given task, return an empty list when nothing depends on it, and throw TASK_NOT_FOUND when the task does not exist.

rule:task-stale-execute.title: task_stale.execute() queries stale tasks with project filter
rule:task-stale-execute.rule: The task_stale.execute() function must call index.getStaleTasks() and pass the project filter parameter through to retrieve tasks exceeding the staleness threshold.

rule:task-stats-execute.title: task_stats.execute() returns stats with project filter
rule:task-stats-execute.rule: The task_stats.execute() function must call index.getStats() and pass the project filter parameter to return aggregated statistics for the specified project.

rule:derive-slug.title: deriveSlug normalizes strings to URL-safe identifiers
rule:derive-slug.rule: The deriveSlug function must lowercase input, strip non-slug characters, replace underscores with hyphens, and trim leading/trailing hyphens to produce valid URL-safe identifiers.

rule:humanise-slug.title: humaniseSlug converts slugs to title case
rule:humanise-slug.rule: The humaniseSlug function must title-case each word separated by hyphens, handle single-word inputs, and gracefully handle empty strings.

rule:extract-title.title: extractTitle extracts H1 from markdown
rule:extract-title.rule: The extractTitle function must extract and trim H1 content from markdown, fall back to humaniseSlug when no H1 is present, truncate at 200 characters, and ignore deeper heading levels.

rule:extract-why.title: extractWhy extracts purpose paragraph from markdown
rule:extract-why.rule: The extractWhy function must return the first paragraph after H1, handle body-only-headings and metadata-only cases, truncate at 500 characters, strip blockquote markers, and return empty string when the first real paragraph is only metadata.

rule:infer-type.title: inferType classifies task type from slug and title
rule:infer-type.rule: The inferType function must classify task types (feature, bug, chore) by slug prefix matching (feature-, implement-, fix-, cleanup-), title matching, and suffix matching (-spec, -plan).

rule:infer-priority.title: inferPriority determines task priority from slug
rule:infer-priority.rule: The inferPriority function must assign high priority to hotfix and critical prefixes, assign high to fix-prefixed tasks, and default to medium priority for other patterns.

rule:infer-status.title: inferStatus determines task status from git and mtime
rule:infer-status.rule: The inferStatus function must infer done status from merged branches with high confidence, use medium confidence for merge-only detection, infer in_progress from existing branches or recent modification times, and avoid false-positive done inference

rule:find-matching-branch.title: findMatchingBranch uses multiple matching strategies
rule:find-matching-branch.rule: The findMatchingBranch function must match branches by exact name, then by feature/<slug>/feat/<slug>/fix/<slug> prefixes, fall back to LCS similarity at 70% threshold for longer slugs (≥12 chars), and return undefined when no branches exist.

rule:extract-pr-number.title: extractPrNumber parses PR numbers from git messages
rule:extract-pr-number.rule: The extractPrNumber function must extract PR numbers from standard GitHub merge messages, return undefined when no PR number is found, and handle multi-digit PR numbers.

rule:build-inference.title: buildInference creates task frontmatter with validation
rule:build-inference.rule: The buildInference function must produce complete frontmatter with all required fields, tag low-confidence items with 'needs_review', and preserve round-trip compatibility with gray-matter using schema_version === 1.

rule:reconcile-legacy-integration.title: reconcileLegacy migration processes scratchpad files
rule:reconcile-legacy-integration.rule: The reconcileLegacy tool must support dry-run mode without writing files, create task files with valid frontmatter, skip already-written files on re-invocation, handle missing scratchpads gracefully, distinguish skipped from new candidates, and skip

rule:task-stats-milestone-burndown.title: task_stats milestone burndown tracks completion
rule:task-stats-milestone-burndown.rule: The task_stats function must correctly count done tasks per milestone (e.g., milestones[0].done === 2 for v2.0) and identify orphaned milestones without associated tasks.

rule:task-claim-validate.title: task_claim.validate() enforces required and type constraints
rule:task-claim-validate.rule: The task_claim.validate() function must require id, enforce ttl_hours as a number when provided, and pass validation when only id is supplied.

rule:task-claim-execute.title: task_claim.execute() manages task claims with TTL
rule:task-claim-execute.rule: The task_claim.execute() function must return claimed=true on successful claim, claimed=false when already claimed by another session, and pass ttl_hours to the store for expiration management.

rule:task-release-validate.title: task_release.validate() requires id
rule:task-release-validate.rule: The task_release.validate() function must require an id field and throw when it is missing.

rule:task-release-execute.title: task_release.execute() releases session claims
rule:task-release-execute.rule: The task_release.execute() function must return released=true on successful release and released=false when the task was not claimed by the current session.

rule:task-transition-validate.title: task_transition.validate() enforces valid status values
rule:task-transition-validate.rule: The task_transition.validate() function must require id, validate that to_status is one of the defined valid statuses, and pass for all valid status values.

rule:task-transition-execute.title: task_transition.execute() delegates to store
rule:task-transition-execute.rule: The task_transition.execute() function must call store.transitionTask() with id and status, pass the optional reason parameter, and propagate INVALID_TRANSITION errors from the store.

rule:task-add-subtask-validate.title: task_add_subtask.validate() enforces required fields and limits
rule:task-add-subtask-validate.rule: The task_add_subtask.validate() function must require parent_id and title, enforce the 200-character title limit, and pass validation only when all constraints are satisfied.

rule:task-add-subtask-execute.title: task_add_subtask.execute() adds subtask with limits
rule:task-add-subtask-execute.rule: The task_add_subtask.execute() function must add a subtask to the parent task and return it, throw TASK_NOT_FOUND when the parent does not exist, and throw INVALID_FIELD when the parent already has 10 subtasks.

rule:task-promote-subtask-validate.title: task_promote_subtask.validate() requires parent and subtask IDs
rule:task-promote-subtask-validate.rule: The task_promote_subtask.validate() function must require both parent_id and subtask_id fields, throwing when either is missing.

rule:task-promote-subtask-execute.title: task_promote_subtask.execute() promotes with depth limit
rule:task-promote-subtask-execute.rule: The task_promote_subtask.execute() function must promote a subtask to a full task and return promoted_task_id, throw TASK_NOT_FOUND when parent or subtask does not exist, and throw MAX_DEPTH_EXCEEDED when a grandparent already exists.

rule:valid-transitions.title: VALID_TRANSITIONS define allowed state machine paths
rule:valid-transitions.rule: The VALID_TRANSITIONS constant must define that todo can go to in_progress or blocked, in_progress can go to done/blocked/todo, done can only go to in_progress, and archived has no valid transitions.

rule:is-valid-transition.title: isValidTransition validates state changes
rule:is-valid-transition.rule: The isValidTransition function must return true for allowed state transitions and false for invalid transitions.

rule:mcp-tasks-error.title: McpTasksError carries error codes and messages
rule:mcp-tasks-error.rule: The McpTasksError class must carry an error code and message and support all defined error codes in the system.

rule:type-contracts-compile-time.title: Type contracts validate union shapes
rule:type-contracts-compile-time.rule: The type contracts must ensure TaskStatus, TaskType (including 'plan'), and Priority unions are correctly defined at compile time.

rule:new-optional-fields-on-task.title: Task schema includes optional tracking fields
rule:new-optional-fields-on-task.rule: The Task type must support optional fields: milestone, estimate_hours, plan_file, auto_captured, labels, and references. TaskReference, Milestone, and CaptureEvent must have correct shapes.

rule:header-stats-wiring.title: Header component uses stats hook and inbox tab
rule:header-stats-wiring.rule: The Header component must import and use the useStats hook and include an inbox tab for navigating to the inbox view.

rule:inbox-view-wired.title: InboxView component supports draft task management
rule:inbox-view-wired.rule: The InboxView component must exist, fetch draft tasks, be rendered by App.tsx for the inbox tab, and provide a promote action to move drafts to backlog.

rule:milestone-crud-ui.title: RoadmapView supports milestone creation
rule:milestone-crud-ui.rule: The RoadmapView must export and use a createMilestone API function, include a New Milestone button and form, and post to /api/milestones endpoint.

rule:ui-type-alignment-backend.title: UI types remain synchronized with backend definitions
rule:ui-type-alignment-backend.rule: The UI TaskStatus, TaskType, and Priority enums must match their backend counterparts. UI must not introduce extraneous statuses, and BoardView must initialize tasks with 'todo' not 'queued'.

rule:commands.title: Commands
rule:commands.rule: - `npm run build` — Compile TypeScript to `dist/` (ESM + CJS via tsup)

rule:write-protocol.title: Write protocol:
rule:write-protocol.rule: SQLite transaction commits first, then markdown atomic rename, then index.yaml atomic rename. If the process dies between steps, the next reconcile corrects it.

rule:mcp-tools-20.title: MCP tools (20):
rule:mcp-tools-20.rule: task-create, task-get, task-list, task-update, task-delete, task-search, task-next, task-claim, task-release, task-transition, task-add-subtask, task-promote-subtask, task-link-commit, task-link-pr, task-link-branch, task-blocked-by, task-unblocks, t

rule:handbook-navigation.title: Handbook Navigation
rule:handbook-navigation.rule: This project uses the handbook tool to maintain a structured knowledge graph.

rule:sanitize-content.title: sanitizeContent redaction rules
rule:sanitize-content.rule: Sanitizes transcript content before sending to AI models by redacting sensitive credentials and tokens (OpenAI/Anthropic-style API keys prefixed with sk-, JWT tokens with three base64url segments, and email addresses) while leaving plain text unmodif

rule:build-prompt.title: buildPrompt message and character budgeting
rule:build-prompt.rule: Constructs prompts with system message and <TRANSCRIPT_START>/<TRANSCRIPT_END> delimiters while enforcing a 40-message cap and 3800-character total budget. Truncates individual oversized messages (>3800 chars) with ellipsis and performs post-truncati

rule:extract-intents-noise-filters.title: extractIntents noise filtering rules
rule:extract-intents-noise-filters.rule: Filters out low-signal transcripts in intent extraction by returning an empty array when the transcript contains fewer than 4 entries or consists entirely of assistant messages, preventing noise in user intent detection.

rule:project-router-normalize-path.title: normalizePath path normalization rules
rule:project-router-normalize-path.rule: normalizePath expands tilde (~) to the user's home directory, removes trailing slashes, resolves paths to absolute form, and lowercases paths on Windows platforms. Must handle cross-platform path handling correctly.

rule:project-router-read-config.title: readConfig configuration file loading with fallback defaults
rule:project-router-read-config.rule: readConfig returns a default configuration when the config file is missing, reads projects array from valid JSON config files, and returns default configuration on JSON parse errors. Must gracefully handle missing and malformed configuration files.

rule:project-router-route-cwd-matching.title: routeProject CWD ancestor matching and specificity selection
rule:project-router-route-cwd-matching.rule: routeProject selects the longest-path (most specific) ancestor when multiple projects match the current working directory, matches when CWD exactly equals a project path, and explicitly does not match projects whose path is merely a string prefix but

rule:project-router-route-prefix-hint.title: routeProject prefix hint matching fallback
rule:project-router-route-prefix-hint.rule: routeProject uses the hint parameter for project selection when CWD does not match any configured project, performs case-insensitive matching against project prefixes, and returns the GEN fallback when the hint does not match any project.

rule:project-router-route-gen-fallback.title: routeProject GEN fallback and lock contention handling
rule:project-router-route-gen-fallback.rule: routeProject triggers the GEN fallback (isGlobal: true) when neither CWD nor hint matches a configured project, skips auto-initialization if a GEN entry already exists in config, and returns null when a lock file held by another process indicates loc

rule:stop-intent-extractor-validation.title: stop-intent-extractor hook validation and silent fallback behavior
rule:stop-intent-extractor-validation.rule: stop-intent-extractor respects the MCP_TASKS_STOP_HOOK_DISABLED environment flag, silently exits with code 0 on empty stdin or malformed JSON, enforces a 512 KB payload size limit, requires a transcript field in the payload, validates transcript entr

rule:binary-resolution-fallback-chain.title: Binary resolution with npm prefix and PATH fallback
rule:binary-resolution-fallback-chain.rule: The resolveBinary and getGlobalNpmPrefix functions implement a fallback chain for locating executables: check npm global prefix directory first, then consult PATH environment variable, with graceful fallback to bare command name if both fail.

rule:path-trusted-check.title: Path validation security guard against traversal
rule:path-trusted-check.rule: The isTrustedPath function validates filesystem paths to prevent directory traversal attacks and ensure paths stay within expected boundaries before processing.

rule:prompt-message-constraints.title: Message character and count budgets for LLM prompts
rule:prompt-message-constraints.rule: PROMPT_CHAR_BUDGET and PROMPT_MSG_CAP define hard limits on total characters and message count passed to LLM inference to prevent context explosion and control token consumption.

rule:content-redaction-sentinel-patterns.title: Regex patterns for redacting sensitive data from prompts
rule:content-redaction-sentinel-patterns.rule: REDACT_PATTERNS is a sentinel list of regex patterns used by sanitizeContent to mask secrets (credentials, tokens, keys) from user-provided text before sending to LLM.

rule:field-level-sanitization.title: Sanitize individual task fields (title, why, spec_file)
rule:field-level-sanitization.rule: The sanitizeField function applies field-specific validation and truncation to task frontmatter fields (enforcing length limits, valid enums, type coercion) before persisting to markdown.

rule:index-yaml-loading.title: Load and parse index.yaml task metadata file
rule:index-yaml-loading.rule: The readIndexYaml function reads the ephemeral index.yaml file (task ID counter, schema version, milestones metadata) and handles parse errors gracefully with fallback defaults.

rule:gen-project-auto-initialization.title: Auto-initialize GEN global project on first use
rule:gen-project-auto-initialization.rule: The initGenProject function automatically creates a default GEN project at ~/.mcp-tasks if no project is found by routing, ensuring a fallback for projects without explicit configuration.

rule:manual-config-write-strategy.title: Atomic config file write via temp-file rename
rule:manual-config-write-strategy.rule: The manualWriteGenConfig function writes configuration files atomically using temp-file rename to prevent partial writes if the process dies mid-write.

rule:aggregated-hint-computation.title: Compute aggregated routing hint from config and state
rule:aggregated-hint-computation.rule: The computeAggregatedHint function combines detected project prefix, explicit config hints, and state from prior routing attempts to produce a single authoritative routing hint for project resolution.

rule:resolve-agent-tasks-binary.title: Resolve agent-tasks executable with npm prefix fallback
rule:resolve-agent-tasks-binary.rule: The resolveAgentTasksBinary function locates the mcp-agent-tasks CLI executable by checking npm global prefix and PATH, enabling the hook to invoke task operations without hardcoded paths.

rule:stop-intent-extractor-hook-validati.title: stop-intent-extractor hook validation and silent fallback behavior
rule:stop-intent-extractor-hook-validati.rule: The stop-intent-extractor hook processes Stop events from the CLI with comprehensive silent fallback behavior. It exits with code 0 for disabled hook (MCP_TASKS_STOP_HOOK_DISABLED=1), empty stdin, malformed JSON, payloads exceeding 512KB (N3 guard),

intent:phase-0-types-schema.title: Phase 0: Types, Interfaces, and Schema Foundation

intent:phase-2-vite-bootstrap.title: Phase 2: Bootstrap Vite + React + Tailwind UI Subproject

intent:phase-3-react-dashboard-lift.title: Phase 3: Lift Legacy Dashboard into React Components

intent:phase-4-vite-cutover.title: Phase 4: Wire serve-ui and Root Build to Vite Output

intent:config-global-write-path.title: Update Config Init to Write Global Config

intent:tailwind-dto-tech-debt.title: Tailwind v4 Compatibility & DTO Type Duplication

intent:silent-error-suppression.title: Silent error suppression in catch blocks creates undiagnosed failures

intent:code-duplication-signal-handlers.title: Duplicated shutdown logic across signal handler callbacks

intent:unused-api-parameters.title: Unused parameters indicate incomplete or evolving API design

intent:sqlite-schema-migration-limitatio.title: SQLite cannot alter CHECK constraints; database rebuild required for constraint changes

intent:schema-version-migration-strategy.title: SCHEMA_VERSION hardcoded constant affects migration and backwards compatibility

intent:reconciliation-silent-skipping.title: Reconciliation silently skips corrupt files instead of failing fast

change:defer-reconcile-background.title: fix: defer reconcile to background with per-project setImmediate chunking
